# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Test

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build-and-test:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Cache node_modules
        id: cache-node-modules
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-modules-${{ hashFiles('package*.json') }}

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Cache pip packages
        id: cache-pip-packages
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pypoetry
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-packages-${{ hashFiles('pyproject.toml') }}

      - name: Install poetry dependencies
        run: |
          if [ ! -f "poetry.lock" ]; then
            poetry install --no-interaction
          fi

      - name: Cache config files
        id: cache-config-files
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: ~/.config
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-config-${{ hashFiles('*.env') }}

      # Environment-specific configs
      - name: Load environment-specific config
        run: |
          if [ "${GITHUB_ENV}" ]; then
            export $(cat .env.$GITHUB_BRANCH | xargs)
          fi

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test
